steps.title: Calculate area of square N
steps.content: Square N has a side length of 6 cm. Using the formula for the area of a square, Area = (side)^2, we get: $$A_{\text{square}} = 6^2 = 36\text{ cm}^2$$

steps.title: Add both areas for total
steps.content: Triangle M has area 72 cm^2 and square N has area 36 cm^2. The total area is: $$A_{\text{total}} = 72 + 36 = 108\text{ cm}^2$$ That confirms D is correct.
